reviews.review_msg: Deniz Anginer is a great professor 10/10 would take again

Deniz is a very responsible professor. He's always willing to help his students outside of class. Even though the assignments and final project(currency report) could be a bit challenging, you will learn a lot out of it in the end. If you put effort into his class, you would achieve a satisfactory grade!

A good guy! Solid choice for a professor. His email answers are vague but his office hours are way better and were the most helpful when I did not understand things from class. Makes you do 3 assignments, 1 final currency project, and two exams.
Deniz is a prof who is always willing to help you outside of class. You will get what you put in to the course. Great guy.
does not respond to emails. impossible to reach outside of class time.
The professor was very uncomfortable to work with

The material was easy, but the workload was heavy in a short period of time. The class was run under a strict structure with many rules, particularly regarding participation and phone use. The classroom environment often felt uncomfortable, and student input did not seem fully acknowledged. Students may want to consider carefully before enrolling.
This course is way too stressful and overwhelming, creating an uncomfortable experience.
Worst professor I have ever met at SFU!!!

Hes one of the better profs at SFU but beware he has VERY strict classroom rules such as 
- no cameras pointing at him (so dont stand up ur ipad) 
- No phones at all, esp if it rings
- Be early or u get told off pretty bad

His teaching is verbal so listen. Participation worth 15%. Major group project worth 15%.

She seemed to now know the material well, as she used slides that were directly from the internet and just read them during lectures. I would avoid taking her class unless you have to, most of it will come down to luck and hoping that she just likes you as that will be a large determinant of the group paper mark.

All multiple choice exam and none of the questions come from anything she provides, nor the textbook. It comes down to guessing, and the best part is that if you speak to others in the exam she will not care at all.

Unfortunately I had nobody else in the course to collaborate with on the exam so I didn't do so well.
She is the amazing professor I have ever had. She is so patient and nice in explaining every detail during her class. And she has quizzes, group assignments and the presentation. Very recommend!
Awesome professor. Explains concepts well and only tests you on things covered in class.

Melissa gives amazing lectures that help you understand complex concepts easily. Bonus that her lectures are engaging and easy to follow. Her exams are very fair if you attend class. Quizzes help lots with what to expect on exams. Would recommend.
great class, really nice prof and always willing to help. Any finance student I suggest taking this class

Really enjoyed attending Professor Gatev's lectures. Has some good insight and perspectives on the current climate in the global business world. Lectures aren't mandatory to succeed but definitely recommend attending. Great course to have a better idea on current events in the financial industry.

horrible. First day of class and the first thing he says is " I am not going to teach you anything, read the textbook twice before coming to class and discuss amongst your peers". Does not care about the students or their lives outside of this course. Avoid at all costs.

I like how it's discussion based and I learned a lot from talking about the news and random Finance facts. But for exams, he gives you a study guide, and then asked questions that are not even on the study guide. SO WHATS THE POINT? He is a good guy but i studied so hard following the guide and he ended up asking something else.........

Teaching styling isn't structured & hard to follow. He explains a lot of math that isn't needed in exams. MT is take home (strongly advise you to use the txtbook not lecture notes). 2nd half of class is group pre. Final based on preMT and group pre. results. Strongly advise u to follow txtbook instead of trying to understand his lectures.
Her attitudes to students are sometimes very insulting when you have questions to ask her. Personally I do not recommend to take her classes. Her hand writing is also very hard to read sometimes.
tests are so hard, nothing related with notes or lectures!

The very beginning of this course is easy, but as this course progresses, it does get harder. Basically, if you have forgotten what you have learned in Bus 316, you will need to put in the extra effort to do well in this course. This is a difficult course so you need to study hard. Her quizzes are easy, her tests are difficult, so look out!
you learn a lot form her class

100% Son of ****. Although her stuff is very easy, she never explain them well. What she is doing is just copying the notes and project them she prepared many many years ago. She can never teach in an efficient manner. She love to embrass the students but actually no one agree with her. Purely ****!!!

It's great to see a prof who truly cares for her students.  Very very dedicated.  Only downside is that she writes down her notes on the overhead and her printing isn't the best of the best.  But that's easily forgiven given how much she helps out her students in class.

Taking Bus 418 with her. I think she should have been huged more as a kid. She is brutal. Not so knowledgable. She makes me feel uncomfortable in class. Hard midterm. I dont know where she gets the questions from. She also tends to focus more on oriental students. I guess she just likes her own race better. BAAAAAD prof.

she is unacceptable!!!! don't take her course. if i had choice, i wouldn't have taken it. an example of her comment; 'i am an instructor, i said wrong, so you are wrong' no one could be worse!! i agree

the worst prof i ever had... a agree about avaoiding her at all cost. Very bad teaching style, unfriendly to students. Lectures make you want to get out from there. Exams questions only marginally related to what has been discussed in class
Her way of teaching is just so so but she is really very very helpful if you approach her for assistance.
A tough course but well worth the effort. Instructor Sumeke was hard drivin' but fair.
got fooled by her great attitude towards students! should have known better, the final exam was a killer!!!

Carlos is one of the best teachers I had at SFU. He is funny and is very entertaining and knowledgeable, although he does talk fast at times but if you ask him he will repeat it for you or slow down. He is very helpful if you need help he will help. Awesome teacher!

Carlos is honestly a great prof. I had him for two courses. He gives great explanations and knows the content well. He also is funny so the lectures stay interesting. Despite being in a class with 30+ people, I felt like he was super open and personal with us all. The class is not an easy A but it is a relaxed class.

Just finished his class and I thought he was ok.  At one point I think he was rated one of the worst profs in the University: http://www.the-peak.ca/article/19835    DO NOT BUY THE RECOMMENDED TEXT BOOK FOR THIS CLASS, he uses his own notes and website.  The exams I thought were pretty fair, mix of hard and easy questions, his grading too.
WORST PROF EVER!!!Avoid him at all cost
Hard final. Similar to practice problems but some of the questions are harder.

worst prof ever. stupid notes. where the hell did he learn to write notes like this? don't know what the heck i am studying. lectures make me fall asleep. it's like the worst 3 hours of my week every single week. what the hell is 60 cent? seriously, what a joke!!! waste of time and money. get ready for some BS if you wanna take his class.
There is not the world of "respect" in his dictionary. He is really offensive and very easy to be angry. Worst professor ever!!
I don understand why he is so cocky and offensive to the students. If you hate us, plz, plz leave the school and find another job. Horrible experience......

Worst prof in my 4 years university studies. He simply can't teach. Cannot explain the material at all. Make stupid joke that just confused students even more. Notes are useless. Does not really use the text book. He is very unorganized.

His notes are very very very very hard to follow! His explanations sometimes don't lead to the conclusions he is trying to make or just don't make sense. Expect to self-study a lot, don't rely only on his lectures and notes because you will only be confused.
Nobody finished his final, period.
Lectures are sooo boring and useless. Has an accent and keeps talking about "60 cents". Avoid taking this class with him.

His notes is exactly the same as the way he talks, which is to split a single sentence into 5 incomplete ones. Had to delete all the #, * and spaces from his notes to actually read it. Does not use the textbook at all and his attitude toward students is extremely offensive.
Prof is so good, and guarnateed A if you attend lecture. He makes things that are so simple complicated so it feels like you learn something.

He spends a lot of time explaining stuff that is logical or is common sense. He has a pretentious attitude toward students and has a tremendous amount of difficulty explaining things. I actually had to go to his office hours for clarity, which I never had to do for any of my other courses.

extremely disappointing class, I was expecting to learn about Security analysis, wasnt the case. All he did was have us use excel, didnt explain anything about it and  expected us to finish it at home with no reference to how to complete the assignments. Seems like he made up the class as he went.
